title: Implementing a Simple Book Recommendation Queue
statement:
Design a RESTful API that accepts book recommendations and stores them in a queue. Then, have a separate endpoint that retrieves the next available book recommendation from the queue. The API should handle errors, invalid input, and edge cases. Please note that this is an in-memory queue and will reset after each restart. You are required to implement the following endpoints:

### POST /queue
- Accepts a book recommendation in the request body.
- Creates a new entry in the queue with the book title, author, and publisher.

### GET /queue
- Retrieves the next available book recommendation from the queue.
- If the queue is empty, returns a 204 No Content status code.
- If the queue is full (10 entries), returns a 429 Too Many Requests status code.

### GET /queue/size
- Retrieves the current size of the queue.

### GET /queue/all
- Retrieves all entries in the queue.

### GET /health
- A health check endpoint to verify the API is operating correctly.

You can use a mock queue in-memory array provided by the starter code. You don't need to store the data persistently.

Please ensure to handle edge cases, errors, and invalid input correctly.

visible test cases will be provided for the following scenarios:

- Valid book recommendation
- Queue retrieval
- Queue size
- Queue retrieval when empty
- Queue retrieval when full
- Error cases (e.g., invalid input, missing headers)

hidden test cases will be provided to check edge cases, error states, and bad input formats.

You are required to write your own mock queue, and please do not use a library for the queue implementation.
constraints: Must use only the in-memory array or mock database provided
Do not use any external libraries for the queue implementation

endpoints.description: Creates a new entry in the queue with the book title, author, and publisher.
Retrieves the next available book recommendation from the queue.
Retrieves the current size of the queue.
Retrieves all entries in the queue.
A health check endpoint to verify the API is operating correctly.
